messages.content:
What is edge computing?


moving large amounts of data closer to the center of the Cloud


making decisions based on predetermined high-level data forecasts


using “cutting-edge“ technology to create more powerful computers


putting computing closer to the users and devices creating data


I don't know this yet.

Edge computing refers to the practice of placing computing resources closer to the data source or user device instead of relying solely on centralized cloud infrastructure. It involves processing and analyzing data at or near the edge of the network, often at or near the source of data generation. This approach reduces latency, optimizes bandwidth usage, enhances real-time capabilities, and provides more efficient and timely responses for various applications and services.

What is edge computing?


moving large amounts of data closer to the center of the Cloud


making decisions based on predetermined high-level data forecasts


using “cutting-edge“ technology to create more powerful computers


putting computing closer to the users and devices creating data


I don't know this yet.

Edge computing refers to the practice of processing and analyzing data closer to the edge of the network, where the data is generated, rather than solely relying on centralized cloud infrastructure. It involves placing computing resources closer to the users and devices that are creating data. This approach helps reduce latency, improves real-time capabilities, optimizes bandwidth usage, and enables more efficient and timely responses for various applications and services.

A large gambling company needs to be able to accept high volumes of customer wagers within short timeframes for high-profile sporting events. Additionally, strict laws prohibit any gambling activities outside of specially-licensed business zones.

What is an example of an effective, elastic Cloud solution that can meet this client's needs?


a mobile app that only accepts wagers based on the user's location


a machine that generates paper betting slips that are brought to a cashier


on-site computer terminals where customers can line up to place bets


a chatbot with which customers can interact to place bets virtually


I don't know this yet.

An effective, elastic cloud solution that can meet the needs of the large gambling company while adhering to strict laws would be a mobile app that only accepts wagers based on the user's location. By using geolocation technology, the app can ensure that bets are accepted only within the specially-licensed business zones where gambling activities are permitted. This solution allows the company to accept high volumes of customer wagers within short timeframes, as users can conveniently place bets using their mobile devices without the need for physical terminals or paper betting slips.

An Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) provider shares its computing resources between a number of different clients, each of whom has a separate dedicated instance to host their own operating systems and applications.

Which Cloud computing concept is illustrated here?


elasticity


multitenancy


multi-regional support


resiliency


I don't know this yet.

The Cloud computing concept illustrated in the given scenario is multitenancy. In multitenancy, an Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) provider shares computing resources among multiple clients, with each client having their dedicated instance to host their operating systems and applications. This allows for efficient resource utilization, cost-sharing, and scalability. Each client's data and applications are isolated and protected from other clients, ensuring security and privacy.

What is a Cloud-first strategy?


a wearable technology that provides customers with on-the-spot personalized experiences


a hybrid Cloud service that allows multiple customers to share control of applications


a service that enhances and automates a business' customer acquisition strategy


a multi-service approach that re-platforms global businesses with greater speed and value


I don't know this yet.

A Cloud-first strategy refers to an organizational approach where businesses prioritize the use of Cloud computing resources and services for their IT infrastructure and operations. It involves considering the Cloud as the primary option for deploying applications, storing data, and running various business processes. This strategy emphasizes leveraging the benefits of the Cloud, such as scalability, flexibility, cost-effectiveness, and easy access to innovative technologies. It aims to maximize the advantages offered by the Cloud in terms of agility, efficiency, and competitive advantage.

What is a Cloud-first strategy?


a wearable technology that provides customers with on-the-spot personalized experiences


a hybrid Cloud service that allows multiple customers to share control of applications


a service that enhances and automates a business' customer acquisition strategy


a multi-service approach that re-platforms global businesses with greater speed and value


I don't know this yet.

A Cloud-first strategy refers to an organizational approach where businesses prioritize the use of Cloud computing resources and services for their IT infrastructure and operations. It involves considering the Cloud as the primary and preferred option for deploying applications, storing data, and running various business processes. In a Cloud-first strategy, businesses aim to leverage the benefits of the Cloud, such as scalability, flexibility, cost-effectiveness, and access to innovative technologies. This approach enables organizations to enhance agility, optimize resources, and focus on their core competencies while relying on the Cloud for various IT needs.

Activity spikes during the holiday season and the sales put significant stress on a major retailer's website.

What feature of Cloud computing would resolve this problem?


multitenancy


elasticity


myNav


blockchain


I don't know this yet.

The feature of Cloud computing that would help resolve the issue of activity spikes and stress on a major retailer's website during the holiday season is elasticity. Elasticity refers to the ability of Cloud computing resources to scale up or down based on demand. In this scenario, the retailer can leverage the elasticity of the Cloud to automatically and dynamically allocate additional computing resources to handle the increased traffic and workload during peak periods, such as the holiday season. This ensures that the website can handle the surge in customer activity without being overwhelmed, providing a smoother and uninterrupted shopping experience for customers.

A client is looking to maximize value from its Cloud investments by baselining, profiling, and prioritizing its human capital needs.

Which myNav module will help this client?


Cloud Business Advisor


Cloud Advisor


Talent Advisor


Green Cloud Advisor


I don't know this yet.

The myNav module that would help the client in baselining, profiling, and prioritizing its human capital needs to maximize value from its Cloud investments is the Talent Advisor module. The Talent Advisor module is specifically designed to assist organizations in aligning their human capital strategy with their Cloud initiatives. It helps in identifying and assessing the skills and capabilities required for successful Cloud adoption, determining skill gaps, and prioritizing human resource investments accordingly. This module provides guidance and recommendations on talent acquisition, development, and management to support the client's Cloud transformation journey effectively.

Which characteristic of Cloud computing allows data centers to better manage hard drive failures and allocate computing resources to users efficiently?


bleeding-edge security


rapid elasticity


measured services


resource pooling


I don't know this yet.

The characteristic of Cloud computing that allows data centers to better manage hard drive failures and allocate computing resources efficiently to users is resource pooling. Resource pooling refers to the practice of combining and efficiently utilizing computing resources, such as processing power, storage, and memory, across multiple users or tenants within the Cloud infrastructure. By pooling resources, data centers can distribute and allocate computing resources dynamically based on demand, allowing for efficient utilization and optimal performance. In the event of a hard drive failure, data centers can seamlessly redistribute resources and workload to other available drives or servers, minimizing disruption and maintaining service availability.

How has the COVID-19 pandemic affected businesses' relationship to Cloud computing?


It has made businesses more reliant on locally-hosted technology solutions.


It has decreased the performance gap between high and low Cloud adopters.


It has made it less important to move to the Cloud, due to decreased activity.


It has accelerated the urgency for businesses to move to the Cloud quickly.


I don't know this yet.

The COVID-19 pandemic has accelerated the urgency for businesses to move to the Cloud quickly. As the pandemic forced many businesses to move to remote work models, the need for scalable, accessible, and collaborative technology solutions became paramount. Cloud computing provides the flexibility and agility required for remote work, enabling employees to access applications, data, and services from anywhere with an internet connection. The pandemic highlighted the limitations of traditional on-premises solutions and increased the demand for Cloud-based solutions to support remote work, collaboration, and business continuity. Therefore, the COVID-19 pandemic has fueled the urgency for businesses to adopt Cloud computing rapidly.

A chain of dry-cleaning outlets wants to improve its operations by using data from devices at individual locations to make real-time adjustments to service delivery.

Which technology would the business combine with its current Cloud operations to make this possible?


Data Visualization


Edge Computing


Blockchain


Public Cloud


I don't know this yet.

To enable real-time adjustments to service delivery using data from devices at individual locations, the business would combine its current Cloud operations with edge computing technology. Edge computing involves processing and analyzing data at or near the edge of the network, closer to the data source. By deploying edge computing devices or infrastructure at each dry-cleaning outlet, the business can collect and process data from local devices in real-time without having to send it to a centralized cloud data center. This enables faster decision-making and immediate adjustments to service delivery based on the data insights. Combining edge computing with existing Cloud operations allows for a distributed and efficient data processing approach that enhances operations and improves service delivery.

Why do many organizations use the hybrid and multi-Cloud approach?


It eliminates the dependency on any private Cloud providers.


It combines the benefits of public and private Cloud providers.


It ensures the business is completely dependent on a single provider.


It allows businesses to share control of their data with other businesses.


I don't know this yet.

Many organizations use the hybrid and multi-Cloud approach because it combines the benefits of both public and private Cloud providers. 

The hybrid Cloud approach allows organizations to have a combination of on-premises infrastructure and off-premises Cloud services. It provides flexibility and agility by allowing workloads to be distributed between private and public Clouds based on their specific needs. This approach enables organizations to keep sensitive or critical data on private Clouds for security and compliance reasons, while using public Cloud services for scalability, cost-effectiveness, and accessing additional resources and services.

The multi-Cloud approach, on the other hand, involves using multiple Cloud providers (both public and private) simultaneously. This approach gives organizations the freedom to select the best Cloud provider for each specific workload or service, taking into account factors like cost, performance, geographical presence, compliance requirements, and service-level agreements. It helps organizations avoid vendor lock-in and maintain flexibility in utilizing various Cloud resources and services from different providers.

In summary, the hybrid and multi-Cloud approaches allow businesses to leverage the benefits of different Cloud providers, combining the security and control of private Clouds with the scalability and flexibility of public Clouds, ultimately optimizing their IT infrastructure and operations.

Accenture is meeting with a well-known, global financial institution that specializes in private and commercial banking to discuss the migration of its sizable application ecosystem to the Cloud. The client has quickly become overwhelmed with the available choices, complexity, and security.

Which tool helps the client self-assess their Cloud landscape to determine the best solution?


myNav


myConcerto


Work Management System (WMS)


DiPA (Digital People Advisor)


I don't know this yet.

The tool that helps the client self-assess their Cloud landscape to determine the best solution is myNav. myNav is a tool provided by Accenture that assists organizations in assessing their Cloud readiness, defining their Cloud strategy, and determining the right Cloud solutions for their specific needs and challenges. It helps clients navigate the complexities of Cloud adoption by providing guidance, insights, and recommendations based on their current IT landscape, business objectives, and industry requirements. With myNav, the client can assess their existing systems, applications, and infrastructure to determine the most suitable approach for migrating their sizable application ecosystem to the Cloud.

An international fast-food chain is looking for opportunities to leverage the Cloud to gain insights into customer data, including location, and order patterns. The client has engaged Accenture to help create customer-focused experiences, including promotions.

They need Accenture's help with this campaign and to create a unique user experience for all their customers.

Which technology, when partnered with Cloud, would allow this to occur?


Extended Reality


Artificial Intelligence


Edge Computing


Blockchain


I don't know this yet.

When partnered with Cloud, the technology that would allow the international fast-food chain to gain insights into customer data and create a unique user experience is Artificial Intelligence (AI). AI can be integrated with Cloud-based platforms to analyze and process large volumes of customer data, including location and order patterns, in real-time. By leveraging AI algorithms and machine learning capabilities, the client can uncover valuable insights from the data and generate personalized recommendations, promotions, and customer-focused experiences. AI-powered chatbots or virtual assistants can also enhance the user experience by providing personalized interactions and recommendations to customers. Thus, combining Cloud with AI enables the fast-food chain to gain insights and deliver unique customer experiences at scale.
